_provenance: Claude-authored original. DeepSeek not used. No official answer; rubric-scored estimate only.

tasks.promptZh: 假设你是李华。下周六你想邀请你的美国朋友 Anna 来参加你家的端午节聚餐。请用英文写一封邀请信。要点：(1) 聚餐的时间和地点；(2) 你们会做什么（包粽子、看龙舟比赛视频）；(3) 请她回复是否能来。词数 60-90，开头 Dear Anna，署名 Li Hua。
tasks.prompt: You are Li Hua. Next Saturday you want to invite your American friend Anna to a Dragon Boat Festival dinner at your home. Write an invitation in English. Audience: your friend Anna. Purpose: invite her to join the festival meal. Include: (1) the time and place of the dinner; (2) what you will do together, such as wrapping zongzi and watching a dragon boat race video; (3) a polite request for her to reply whether she can come. Word limit 60-90. Begin with 'Dear Anna' and sign as 'Li Hua'.
referencePoints: Invitation format with greeting (Dear Anna) and signature (Li Hua)
Time, place, planned activities and a request to reply all included
Future forms such as 'will' or 'be going to' used correctly
Warm, inviting tone suitable for a friend
Reference points guide scoring only and are not a model answer

tasks.promptZh: 你在公交车上捡到一个黑色钱包，里面有一张学生卡和一些现金。请用英文写一则失物招领启事，贴在学校公告栏上。要点：(1) 你在哪里、什么时候捡到的；(2) 钱包的样子和里面的东西；(3) 失主如何联系你认领。词数 50-80。
tasks.prompt: You found a black wallet on the bus with a student card and some cash inside. Write a lost-and-found notice in English to put on the school noticeboard. Audience: students at your school. Purpose: help the owner get the wallet back. Include: (1) where and when you found it; (2) what the wallet looks like and what is inside; (3) how the owner can contact you to claim it. Word limit 50-80. Use a notice format with a title such as 'Found'.
referencePoints: Notice format used with a clear title (e.g. Found) and contact line
Place, time, description of the wallet and contents all stated
Past tense for finding and present details described accurately
Brief, clear and helpful public tone
Reference points are for scoring guidance, not a fixed answer

tasks.promptZh: 假设你是李华。你的英国笔友 Jack 计划暑假来你的城市旅行，向你打听好去处。请用英文回信，向他介绍你城市的一个旅游景点。要点：(1) 景点的名字和位置；(2) 那里有什么好玩或值得看的；(3) 给他一条实用的小建议（如带伞、穿舒适的鞋）。词数 60-90，开头 Dear Jack，署名 Li Hua。
tasks.prompt: You are Li Hua. Your British pen friend Jack plans to travel to your city this summer and asks you for good places to visit. Write a reply in English to introduce one tourist spot in your city. Audience: your pen friend Jack. Purpose: recommend a place and help him plan. Include: (1) the name and location of the place; (2) what is fun or worth seeing there; (3) one practical tip for him, such as bringing an umbrella or wearing comfortable shoes. Word limit 60-90. Begin with 'Dear Jack' and sign as 'Li Hua'.
referencePoints: Letter format with greeting (Dear Jack) and signature (Li Hua)
Name, location, attractions and one practical tip all covered
Present tense and recommending language used correctly
Helpful, enthusiastic tone suitable for a friend
Reference points guide scoring only and are not a model answer

tasks.prompt: Your class is making an English wall newspaper on the topic 'My Good Habit'. Write a short passage in English about one good habit of yours.
